4.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
4.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

10.gl: properly, royalty, i.e. (abstractly) rule, or (concretely) a realm (literally or figuratively)

11.gl: the sky; by extension, heaven (as the abode of God); by implication, happiness, power, eternity; specially, the Gospel (Christianity)
11.dv: perhaps from the same as G3735 (ὄρος) (through the idea of elevation);
